theme: Price a product to spark network adoption
title: Cascade Pricing: Igniting Network Adoption Across Regions
strategies: flat pricing: charge every region the same revenue-maximizing single price -- reproduces the evaluator's network-blind, region-blind floor (~0.1)

myopic per-region pricing (the trap): maximize each region's own revenue in isolation, assuming v_i = base_i and ignoring the influence network W entirely -- prices high-downstream-influence regions for their own local demand curve, never sacrificing margin, so the cascade that a coordinated price could ignite in dependent regions never fires

cascade-igniting pricing (the insight): rank regions by downstream revenue-weighted influence sum_j gamma_j*W[j][i]*pop_j, sacrifice the top few below their own myopic price (toward zero) to push their adoption toward 1 through the best-response equilibrium, then re-price every other region upward against the network-boosted value the cascade now delivers -- trying a small set of (how many regions, how deep) choices and keeping the best simulated total revenue

full coordinate-ascent search: joint per-region price refinement from multiple sacrifice-depth starting points -- captures the residual gains from fine-tuning follower prices after the cascade ignites, approaching but (after the evaluator's headroom rescaling) never reaching the score ceiling
